page: Amazon Web Services m6id.24xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 96 dedicated vCPUs, consisting of 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, utilizing Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of 5700 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 38 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory performance. The server achieved a strong rating in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench multi-core tests. Memory bandwidth tests show strong performance, reaching 4972.07 GB/sec for small block cached reads and 329.31 GB/sec for large block uncached reads. Cryptographic capabilities are notable, with the Passmark CPU encryption test placing in the top tier. Single-core performance remains average across stress-ng, Geekbench, and compression tests.

While the server excels in multi-threaded tasks, software compilation, and database operations, it exhibits limitations in large-scale machine learning tasks. Large language model inference for text generation using a 70B model is weak, yielding only 2.58 tokens per second. However, prompt processing for smaller models remains strong. Given the high local NVMe storage capacity and balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio, this instance is highly suitable for database operations, static web serving, software development pipelines, and general-purpose enterprise workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services m6id.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs from Intel Xeon 8375C processors running at 3.5 GHz, paired with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory. It includes 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD instance storage and a 38 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks show strong multi-core processing, high memory bandwidth, and top-tier cryptographic execution. However, single-core performance is average, and large-scale LLM text generation is weak. This hardware profile offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring balanced compute and fast local storage, such as database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.24xlarge is a general-purpose server with 96 Intel vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 5700 GB local NVMe SSD storage, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6id.24xlarge features 96 Intel vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 5700 GB NVMe SSD storage for database and general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity general-purpose server combining 96 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs with fast local NVMe SSD storage for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs running at 3.5 GHz
384 GB of DDR4 memory and 5700 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Strong multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Top-tier cryptographic performance and strong database operation metrics
Weak text generation performance for large-scale language models
Best suited for database hosting, web serving, and multi-threaded applications
